name: Cocoa powder, unsweetened (food medium)

components.name: total protein
components.source: USDA FDC 169593 (SR Legacy, Cocoa, dry powder, unsweetened)
components.evidence: Storage-protein-rich; principal N and (with organic acids) a principal C source for growth on cocoa.

components.name: starch / non-sugar digestible carbohydrate
components.source: USDA FDC 169593 (carbohydrate by difference 57.9 g, fiber 37.0 g, sugars 1.7 g -> ~19 g residual incl. starch, oligosaccharides, organic acids)
components.evidence: Approximate; cocoa nib starch ~4-9%. Value is order-of-magnitude, medium-low confidence.

components.name: sucrose
components.source: USDA FDC 169593 (total sugars 1.7 g); Fang 2020 doi:10.1002/fsn3.1701 (sucrose largely hydrolysed during fermentation)
components.evidence: Free sugars are MINOR in fermented/roasted cocoa; sucrose partly consumed. Split of the 1.7 g total is approximate.

components.name: D-fructose
components.source: USDA FDC 169593 (total sugars 1.7 g); Fang 2020 doi:10.1002/fsn3.1701 (fructose > glucose among free reducing sugars in fermented cocoa)
components.evidence: Reducing sugar liberated by sucrose hydrolysis; approximate split of total sugars.

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: USDA FDC 169593 (total sugars 1.7 g); Fang 2020 doi:10.1002/fsn3.1701
components.evidence: Reducing sugar; approximate split of total sugars.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Holm et al. 1993 J Sci Food Agric doi:10.1002/jsfa.2740610111 (fermented beans 1.6-9.9 g/kg)
components.evidence: Dominant NON-volatile organic acid of cocoa; carried into the defatted powder. Midpoint estimate, medium confidence.

components.name: acetic acid
components.source: Holm et al. 1993 doi:10.1002/jsfa.2740610111 (fermented beans 1.3-11.8 g/kg); Li 2012 doi:10.1016/j.foodres.2011.03.008 (acetic predominant, -67% after heavy alkalization)
components.evidence: Predominant acid before processing but volatile; substantially reduced by roasting/alkalization. Estimate, medium-low confidence; near-zero in heavily Dutched powder.

components.name: lactic acid
components.source: Holm et al. 1993 doi:10.1002/jsfa.2740610111 (fermented beans 0.6-11.1 g/kg)
components.evidence: Non-volatile fermentation acid retained in powder; midpoint estimate, medium confidence.

components.name: oxalic acid
components.source: Ould Zeidane/oxalate study, Food Res Int 2011 doi:10.1016/j.foodres.2011.03.011 (cocoa powder total oxalate 650-783 mg/100g DM, mean 729)
components.evidence: Well-quantified for cocoa powder specifically; ~64% soluble (mean 469 mg/100g). High confidence.

components.name: tartaric acid
components.source: Goude et al. 2023 Food Systems (organic acids detected in cocoa)
components.evidence: Present but minor/unquantified for cocoa powder.

components.name: succinic acid
components.source: Goude et al. 2023 Food Systems; cocoa metabolomics
components.evidence: Present, minor, unquantified.

components.name: malic acid
components.evidence: Minor constituent, unquantified in cocoa powder.

components.name: L-alanine
components.evidence: Protein-bound + free; hydrophobic AA elevated by fermentation.

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Most abundant amino acid in cocoa protein.

components.name: L-leucine
components.evidence: Most abundant essential AA.

components.name: L-phenylalanine
components.evidence: Missing from current FooDB list; added.

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: Current list had D-serine; corrected to L-serine (protein-derived).

components.name: L-valine
components.evidence: Missing from current FooDB list; added.

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: Dominant mineral.

components.name: magnesium
components.evidence: Cocoa is exceptionally Mg-rich.

components.name: sodium
components.evidence: Low in natural powder; higher if Dutch-processed with Na/K carbonate.

components.name: copper
components.evidence: Cocoa is one of the richest dietary Cu sources.

components.name: chloride
components.evidence: Present, unquantified in USDA.

components.name: sulfate (as S, from S-amino acids/sulfate)
components.evidence: Present; keep for medium S source.

components.name: molybdenum
components.evidence: Trace essential micronutrient; unquantified.

components.name: nickel
components.source: FooDB FOOD00685; cocoa is a naturally Ni-rich food
components.evidence: Genuinely elevated in cocoa (kept, not removed as contaminant).

components.name: alpha-tocopherol (vitamin E)
components.evidence: Low.

components.name: choline
components.evidence: Approximate; present.

components.name: palmitic acid (C16:0)
components.source: USDA FDC 169593 (saturated 8.1 g); cocoa butter profile ~26% C16:0
components.evidence: MAJOR cocoa-butter fatty acid MISSING from current list; added.

components.name: stearic acid (C18:0)
components.source: USDA FDC 169593 (saturated 8.1 g); cocoa butter profile ~34% C18:0
components.evidence: Co-dominant cocoa-butter fatty acid MISSING from current list; added.

components.name: oleic acid (C18:1 n-9)
components.evidence: Co-dominant fatty acid.

components.name: theobromine
components.source: Craig & Nguyen 1984; Zoumas et al. (cocoa powder theobromine ~2.0-2.8 g/100g)
components.evidence: DEFINING metabolite of cocoa; the dominant methylxanthine (3-10x caffeine). No BiGG met -> keep_uncovered but quantified.

components.name: caffeine
components.evidence: Defining minor methylxanthine; keep_uncovered, quantified.

components.name: water
components.evidence: Low-moisture powder.

remove.name: Cholesterol
remove.reason: Animal sterol, biologically implausible in a plant-derived cocoa powder; FooDB predicted, not a real cocoa nutrient.

remove.name: L-Ascorbate (vitamin C)
remove.reason: USDA FDC 169593 reports 0 mg vitamin C in cocoa powder; not a growth nutrient here.

remove.name: Vitamin D
remove.reason: USDA reports 0 IU vitamin D; absent in unfortified plant food (FooDB predicted).

remove.name: Cyanocobalamin (B12)
remove.reason: USDA reports 0 ug B12; plant food contains no cobalamin.

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: Animal-form vitamin A; USDA vitamin A = 0 in cocoa powder; implausible in plant food.

remove.name: Beta-Carotene
remove.reason: USDA vitamin-A/carotene activity ~0 in cocoa powder; negligible, not a growth-relevant nutrient.

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+) / Fe2+ mitochondria / Iron (duplicate entries)
remove.reason: Redundant iron speciation/compartment tags from FooDB; consolidated into a single 'iron' component (13.86 mg/100g).

remove.name: Cobalt (Co2+)
remove.reason: Trace non-nutritive element; not a quantified growth nutrient of cocoa (retain only if a Co/B12 source is explicitly required).

remove.name: D-Serine
remove.reason: Corrected to L-serine; D-enantiomer is not the protein-derived form present in cocoa.

missing_added: palmitic acid (C16:0) - major cocoa-butter fatty acid, was absent
stearic acid (C18:0) - co-dominant cocoa-butter fatty acid, was absent
L-valine, L-phenylalanine - abundant amino acids absent from FooDB list
citric acid, acetic acid, lactic acid, oxalic acid - organic acids (fermentation-derived), all quantified
individual free sugars (sucrose/glucose/fructose) split from total
full USDA vitamin & mineral panel with amounts
keep_uncovered: theobromine, caffeine (methylxanthines; defining, no BiGG met)
(-)-epicatechin, (+)-catechin, procyanidin B1/B2/C1, cinnamtannin A2 (major cocoa flavan-3-ols/procyanidins)
chlorogenic/caffeoyl aspartic acid, syringic acid, vanillin, vanillate, benzoate (genuine phenolics/aroma)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 169593 (SR Legacy: Cocoa, dry powder, unsweetened) - full macro/AA/mineral/vitamin/fatty-acid panel
Holm et al. 1993, J Sci Food Agric doi:10.1002/jsfa.2740610111 (organic acids in fermented cocoa)
Oxalate in cocoa powder, Food Res Int 2011 doi:10.1016/j.foodres.2011.03.011 (total 650-783 mg/100g DM)
Li et al. 2012, Food Res Int doi:10.1016/j.foodres.2011.03.008 (acetic acid predominance / alkalization effect)
Fang et al. 2020, Food Sci Nutr doi:10.1002/fsn3.1701 (sugar/AA changes during fermentation)
Goude et al. 2023, Food Systems (cocoa organic-acid/sugar profiling)
notes: Cocoa powder is a fermented-then-roasted, defatted concentrate: dominant growth-relevant carbon/energy sources are its abundant amino acids (protein 19.6 g/100g; Glu, Asp, Leu, Val highest) and organic acids (citric, lactic, acetic, oxalic ~0.7 g), NOT free sugars (only ~1.7 g total). Cocoa butter (13.7 g fat) is chiefly stearic + oleic + palmitic acid. Exceptionally mineral-dense (K, Mg, P, Cu). Organic-acid and individual-sugar amounts are literature midpoint estimates (medium confidence; volatile acetic acid varies strongly with roasting/alkalization); USDA panel values are high confidence. Theobromine is the defining metabolite.
